claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start, I need to know about your child (or children). 

**Could you please tell me:**
1. Do you have a child or children you're wanting to claim Child Benefit for?
2. How old is your child (or if you have multiple children, what are their ages)?

This will help me determine which eligibility path applies to your situation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes, I'm wanting to claim Child Benefit. I have two children - Alex is 8 years old and Blake is 20.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me assess the eligibility for both of your children, Alex (8 years old) and Blake (20 years old).

Let me start with Alex, who is 8 years old (under 16):

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
For **Alex (8 years old)**: Since Alex is under 16, they would typically qualify as a qualifying child. Let me continue checking the requirements.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?** 
(For an 8-year-old, this would typically be yes, but I need to confirm.)

Now, for **Blake (20 years old)**: Since Blake is 20 or over, I need to let you know right away that unfortunately, Child Benefit is generally not available for children aged 20 or over. The age limit for Child Benefit is under 20 (or under 20 in certain circumstances with approved education or training).

Let me confirm the situation with Alex first, and then we can discuss Blake's situation in more detail.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Alex doesn't work at all - he's only 8! 

And yes, I understand Blake is 20, but I wanted to check about both of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: You're absolutely right - of course Alex doesn't work! Let me continue with the assessment for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now, continuing with Alex:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right** (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)? 

(Again, for an 8-year-old this would typically be no benefits, but I need to confirm to complete the assessment properly.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue checking the requirements for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

(For an 8-year-old, this would be no, but let me confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in an apprenticeship - he's only 8 years old!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! I apologize for the repetitive questions - I need to follow the full assessment process. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now we need to check about **your** residency status (the claimant). 

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?** You would qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. You normally live in the UK
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support your family
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You're a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

Which of these applies to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I don't live in the UK - I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, let me ask some follow-up questions to see if you might still qualify:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant** (working for the UK government, such as in the diplomatic service or armed forces) **or living in certain qualifying countries?**

If not, could you tell me a bit more about your situation:
- Which country are you currently living in?
- Are you working abroad temporarily or is this a permanent move?
- Do you have any connection to the UK (such as being ordinarily resident in the UK before moving)?

This information will help me determine if you meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant. I've just moved abroad with my family - it's not a temporary work thing, we've relocated permanently.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Based on what you've told me, since you've permanently relocated abroad and you're not a Crown servant or in one of the special qualifying categories, unfortunately you would not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

Let me provide you with the complete assessment for both of your children:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Complete - Not Eligible

I'm sorry, but based on your circumstances, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake. Here's the breakdown:

### **For Alex (8 years old):**
- ✓ Alex meets the age requirement (under 16)
- ✓ Alex is not working full-time
- ✓ Alex is not receiving benefits in his own right
- ✓ Alex is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- ✗ **However, you do not meet the UK residency requirements**

### **For Blake (20 years old):**
- ✗ Blake is 20 years old, which exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit (under 20)
- ✗ You do not meet the UK residency requirements

---

### **Why You're Not Eligible:**

**UK Residency Requirement:** To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances such as:
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with qualifying criteria
- Being a Crown servant working abroad
- Living in certain qualifying countries

Since you've permanently relocated abroad and don't fall into any of these special categories, you don't meet the residency requirements.

### **Important Information:**

If your circumstances change (for example, if you return to live in the UK), you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit at that time for Alex (as long as he remains under 20 and meets other eligibility criteria).

For more information, you can visit: [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about this decision?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and meets the age requirement (under 16). Alex is not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship, so he qualifies as a 'qualifying child'. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements as they have permanently relocated abroad and are not a Crown servant or in another special qualifying category. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old, which exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility (under 20, or under 20 in approved education/training). Additionally, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (8) and Blake (20). While Alex would qualify as a 'qualifying child' based on age and other child-specific criteria, the claimant does not meet the fundamental UK residency requirements as they have permanently relocated abroad and are not in a special qualifying category (such as Crown servant). Blake, at 20 years old, is over the age limit for Child Benefit regardless of the residency issue. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old, which exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility (under 20, or under 20 in approved education/training). Additionally, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Blake.
